articles.title: RBI Holds Repo Rate Steady at 6.5% Amid Inflation Concerns
articles.excerpt: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee voted unanimously to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%, citing sticky core inflation and global uncertainty.
articles.content: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its three-day meeting on Wednesday with a unanimous decision to hold the repo rate at 6.5%. RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized that while retail inflation has moderated, core inflation remains above the comfort zone. The committee also revised the GDP growth forecast slightly upward to 7.2% for FY2026-27, buoyed by strong services exports and domestic consumption. Das warned that geopolitical tensions and uneven monsoon distribution remain key downside risks.
articles.author: Priya Sharma

articles.title: RBI Introduces New Digital Lending Guidelines for NBFCs
articles.excerpt: The RBI has issued revised guidelines for digital lending platforms operated by NBFCs, requiring stronger borrower consent mechanisms and interest rate disclosures.
articles.content: The Reserve Bank of India released a comprehensive circular updating digital lending norms for non-banking financial companies. The new rules mandate that all loan sanction letters must clearly state the Annual Percentage Rate (APR), total cost of credit, and grievance redressal channels. NBFCs must ensure borrower consent is obtained through a standardized Key Fact Statement before loan disbursement. Violations will attract penalties under the RBI Act. The guidelines take effect from July 1, 2026.
articles.author: Amit Verma

articles.title: RBI's Forex Reserves Hit New High of $695 Billion
articles.excerpt: India's foreign exchange reserves climbed to a record $695 billion as of last week, providing a comfortable import cover of over 12 months, the RBI data showed.
articles.content: India's foreign exchange reserves surged to an all-time high of $695.2 billion for the week ending June 20, 2026, according to data released by the Reserve Bank of India. The jump was driven by a rise in foreign currency assets and gold holdings. The current reserves provide an import cover of approximately 12.4 months, well above the recommended three-month threshold. Analysts attribute the build-up to strong FPI inflows into equity and debt markets amid improving global risk appetite.
articles.author: Neha Joshi

articles.title: Sensex Rallies 600 Points on Strong FII Inflows, IT Stocks Lead
articles.excerpt: Benchmark indices surged Wednesday as foreign institutional investors pumped over Rs 4,200 crore into Indian equities, with IT and banking stocks leading the advance.
articles.content: The BSE Sensex closed 623 points higher at 82,450 while the NSE Nifty50 gained 178 points to settle at 25,112 on Wednesday. Strong foreign institutional investor (FII) buying worth Rs 4,234 crore drove broad-based gains. The IT sector was the top gainer with Infosys, TCS, and Wipro rising 2–3% after positive guidance from a US tech bellwether overnight. Banking stocks also gained as bond yields eased. Midcap and smallcap indices outperformed the benchmarks, each rising over 1.5%.
articles.author: Rahul Mehta

articles.title: Nifty Bank Index Hits Record High as HDFC Bank Surges 4%
articles.excerpt: The Nifty Bank index scaled a fresh all-time high on the back of HDFC Bank's sharp rally after management raised its loan growth guidance for FY27.
articles.content: The Nifty Bank index touched a new all-time high of 54,320 intraday before settling at 54,180, a gain of 1.8% for the day. HDFC Bank shares surged 4.2% after its management at an investor conference projected loan book growth of 14–16% for FY2026-27, up from earlier guidance of 12–14%. ICICI Bank and Axis Bank also rose 2% each in sympathy. Analysts upgraded target prices for HDFC Bank citing improving net interest margins and reduced credit costs.
articles.author: Sunita Rao

articles.title: Delhi Records Hottest June Day in a Decade at 46.2°C
articles.excerpt: The national capital sizzled at 46.2 degrees Celsius on Wednesday, the highest June temperature recorded in Delhi in over a decade, with the IMD issuing a red alert.
articles.content: Delhi experienced its most intense heatwave of the season on Wednesday, with the Safdarjung observatory recording a maximum temperature of 46.2°C — the highest in June since 2012. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued a red alert across Delhi-NCR, advising residents to avoid stepping outdoors between 11 am and 5 pm. Hospitals reported a 30% uptick in heat-related admissions. The Delhi government opened 500 cooling centres across the city. Power demand hit a new peak of 8,400 MW as air-conditioning use surged.
articles.author: Kavita Singh

articles.title: Delhi Floods: Yamuna Rises Above Danger Mark After Heavy Rains Upstream
articles.excerpt: The Yamuna river at Old Delhi Railway Bridge touched 205.8 metres, above the danger mark of 204.5 metres, triggering evacuation of low-lying areas in Delhi.
articles.content: The Yamuna at the Old Delhi Railway Bridge gauge rose to 205.8 metres early Wednesday after heavy rainfall in Himachal Pradesh and Haryana caused excess water release from the Hathnikund barrage. The Delhi administration issued an advisory asking residents of flood-prone areas including Yamuna Khadar, Shastri Nagar, and Garhi Mandu to evacuate. NDRF teams were deployed at six critical locations. Schools within 500 metres of the riverbank were ordered shut until further notice.
articles.author: Ananya Gupta

articles.title: Delhi Government Announces Free Bus Rides for Senior Citizens
articles.excerpt: The Delhi Cabinet approved a scheme offering free DTC and cluster bus rides to all senior citizens above 60 years, effective from July 15, 2026.
articles.content: The Delhi Cabinet on Wednesday cleared a new welfare scheme providing free rides on Delhi Transport Corporation and cluster buses to all residents above 60 years of age. The scheme, to be implemented from July 15, 2026, is expected to benefit approximately 22 lakh senior citizens. Beneficiaries will need to register via a dedicated portal and receive a Smart Commute Card. The annual budgetary outlay for the scheme is estimated at Rs 680 crore. Opposition parties welcomed the move but questioned the funding source.
articles.author: Pooja Nair

articles.title: Bihar By-Elections: Ruling Alliance Wins 4 of 5 Seats, Opposition Holds One
articles.excerpt: Results for the five Bihar assembly by-elections showed the ruling NDA alliance winning four constituencies, while the INDIA bloc retained one seat in Kishanganj.
articles.content: The results of five Bihar assembly by-elections declared Wednesday showed the NDA alliance—comprising the BJP, JDU, and HAM—winning four of the five contested seats. The INDIA bloc managed to retain the Kishanganj constituency, a traditional stronghold. Chief Minister Nitish Kumar termed the results a people's endorsement of the government's development agenda. The by-elections were necessitated by the death of two sitting MLAs and disqualification of three others on defection grounds. Voter turnout averaged 58%, lower than the 2025 assembly elections.
articles.author: Sanjay Pandey

articles.title: Election Commission Issues Notice to Party Over Poll Code Violation
articles.excerpt: The Election Commission of India issued a show-cause notice to a national party for distributing cash at a campaign event ahead of the Rajasthan by-polls scheduled next month.
articles.content: The Election Commission of India (ECI) on Wednesday issued a show-cause notice to a national political party for alleged distribution of cash at a campaign rally in Barmer ahead of the Rajasthan by-elections scheduled for July 28. The notice, based on video evidence collected by a flying squad, asked the party to respond within 48 hours. The ECI has also deployed additional central observer teams in the constituency. This is the third such notice issued by the commission in the current election cycle.
articles.author: Deepak Tiwari

articles.title: Gold Price Rises to Rs 74,500 per 10g on Weak Dollar and Global Demand
articles.excerpt: Gold rates in India climbed to Rs 74,500 per 10 grams on Wednesday as the US dollar weakened and international spot gold prices touched a two-month high.
articles.content: Gold prices in the domestic market rose Rs 350 to Rs 74,500 per 10 grams in Delhi on Wednesday, tracking gains in international markets where spot gold touched $2,420 per troy ounce—a two-month high. The dollar index fell to a six-week low after softer-than-expected US economic data raised hopes of Federal Reserve rate cuts. Domestic demand also improved ahead of the upcoming wedding season. Silver followed gold higher, gaining Rs 500 to Rs 91,200 per kilogram.
articles.author: Vikash Agarwal

articles.title: RBI Allows Banks to Import Gold Directly to Ease Domestic Supply
articles.excerpt: The Reserve Bank of India modified its master circular to permit scheduled commercial banks to import gold directly under the import-on-consignment model.
articles.content: The RBI on Wednesday amended its master circular on import of gold to expand the list of eligible entities allowed to import the yellow metal on consignment basis. The revision permits scheduled commercial banks—beyond the current set of nominated agencies—to import gold directly. The move is aimed at ensuring adequate domestic supply and reducing the premium that Indian buyers pay over international prices. Industry bodies had been seeking the change for over two years. The new framework will be reviewed after six months.
articles.author: Priya Sharma

articles.title: Startup Funding Hits 18-Month High with $3.2B Raised in May 2026
articles.excerpt: Indian startups raised $3.2 billion across 112 deals in May 2026, the highest monthly funding since November 2024, with fintech and SaaS sectors leading.
articles.content: Indian startups secured $3.2 billion across 112 funding deals in May 2026, marking an 18-month high, according to data from Tracxn and Inc42. Fintech led with $980 million raised across 28 deals, followed by SaaS at $620 million. Three new unicorns emerged in the month—a healthtech platform, a B2B supply chain startup, and an agritech company. Late-stage funding dominated with 68% of capital going to Series C and beyond. The strong pipeline is attributed to renewed interest from global venture capital funds that had pulled back in 2024.
articles.author: Riya Desai

articles.title: Zepto Files DRHP for IPO; Plans to Raise Rs 5,000 Crore
articles.excerpt: Quick-commerce platform Zepto filed its draft red herring prospectus with SEBI on Wednesday, seeking approval for an IPO to raise approximately Rs 5,000 crore.
articles.content: Quick-commerce startup Zepto filed its draft red herring prospectus (DRHP) with the Securities and Exchange Board of India on Wednesday, aiming to raise Rs 5,000 crore through a combination of a fresh issue and offer for sale. The company reported a GMV of Rs 24,000 crore in FY2025-26 and narrowed its net loss to Rs 320 crore from Rs 1,200 crore in the previous year. At implied valuation, the IPO would value Zepto at around Rs 80,000 crore ($9.6 billion). Co-founders Aadit Palicha and Kaivalya Vohra will retain majority control post-listing.
articles.author: Manish Kapoor

articles.title: Parliament Passes Digital Personal Data Protection Amendment Bill
articles.excerpt: Both houses of Parliament cleared the Digital Personal Data Protection Amendment Bill 2026, introducing stricter penalties and a new consent management framework.
articles.content: The Digital Personal Data Protection (Amendment) Bill 2026 was passed by Rajya Sabha on Wednesday, a day after it cleared Lok Sabha, completing the legislative process. The bill enhances penalties for data breaches to up to Rs 500 crore per incident, up from Rs 250 crore in the parent act. It also introduces a Consent Management Registry where users can manage data consent with all platforms from a single dashboard. The opposition raised concerns about government exemptions, which the minister said are necessary for national security. The bill will now go for Presidential assent.
articles.author: Asha Krishnamurthy

articles.title: Opposition Walks Out of Lok Sabha Over Manipur Discussion Demand
articles.excerpt: Opposition MPs walked out of Lok Sabha on Wednesday demanding an immediate discussion under Rule 267 on the ongoing situation in Manipur, which the Speaker declined.
articles.content: The Lok Sabha session was disrupted on Wednesday morning after opposition MPs from the INDIA bloc staged a walkout demanding an urgent debate on the security situation in Manipur. The Speaker rejected a Rule 267 notice, stating the house had other urgent legislative business scheduled. The Leader of Opposition moved a motion for adjournment, which was also disallowed. Treasury benches accused the opposition of playing politics while the government maintained the situation in Manipur is improving. Question Hour resumed after the walkout.
articles.author: Vikram Chandra

articles.title: IMD Predicts Above-Normal Monsoon Rainfall for Western India This Week
articles.excerpt: The India Meteorological Department forecast above-normal rainfall over Maharashtra, Goa, and Gujarat for the next five days as the monsoon strengthens over the Arabian Sea.
articles.content: The India Meteorological Department issued a five-day forecast on Wednesday predicting above-normal monsoon rainfall across Maharashtra, Goa, Gujarat, and coastal Karnataka. A well-marked low pressure area developing in the eastern Arabian Sea is expected to intensify into a depression, drawing strong southwesterly winds. Mumbai is forecast to receive 200–250 mm of rainfall in the next 72 hours. Orange alerts have been issued for several districts in Konkan and Ghat regions. Farmers in Maharashtra were advised to complete pending harvests of summer-sown crops.
articles.author: Suresh Pillai

articles.title: Cyclone Alert Issued for Odisha and Andhra Coasts as Bay of Bengal System Intensifies
articles.excerpt: A deep depression in the Bay of Bengal is likely to intensify into a cyclonic storm within 24 hours, prompting alerts for coastal Odisha and north Andhra Pradesh.
articles.content: The India Meteorological Department on Wednesday upgraded the weather system in the west-central Bay of Bengal from a deep depression to a potential cyclonic storm, issuing orange and red alerts for coastal districts of Odisha and north Andhra Pradesh. The system, currently centered 420 km southeast of Paradip, is likely to cross the Odisha coast between Puri and Konark on Friday morning. Fishermen have been advised not to venture into the sea. The National Disaster Response Force has pre-positioned 14 teams in Odisha and 6 in Andhra Pradesh.
articles.author: Meena Reddy

articles.title: Stock Market: Small-Cap Rally Pushes BSE SME Index to Record
articles.excerpt: The BSE SME IPO index hit an all-time high on Wednesday as retail investor appetite for small and micro-cap stocks surged amid a broader market rally.
articles.content: The BSE SME IPO index scaled a fresh record high of 1,42,350 on Wednesday, extending gains for the third straight session. Retail investor interest in small and micro-cap stocks remained robust with daily volumes on the SME platform crossing Rs 3,200 crore. SEBI has been monitoring the segment for signs of overheating and issued an advisory last week cautioning retail investors about the risks of low-liquidity counters. Analysts say the rally reflects broader market optimism but caution about thin institutional coverage in the SME segment.
articles.author: Neeraj Gupta

articles.title: Bengaluru Startup BrightMind AI Raises $45M Series B for EdTech Platform
articles.excerpt: Bengaluru-based AI-powered tutoring startup BrightMind AI closed a $45 million Series B round co-led by Sequoia India and Lightspeed Venture Partners.
articles.content: BrightMind AI, a Bengaluru-based artificial intelligence-powered K-12 tutoring platform, announced the close of a $45 million Series B funding round on Wednesday. The round was co-led by Sequoia India and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with participation from existing investor Matrix Partners India. The startup will use proceeds to expand to 10 new Indian cities, develop vernacular-language content in six regional languages, and triple its engineering team. BrightMind AI currently serves 1.8 million students across Tier-1 and Tier-2 cities and claims a 73% improvement in test scores among regular users.
articles.author: Shalini Mehta
